design_philosophy.core_loop: Combat-focused platforming with weapon pickups and health management across linear level progression
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ammunition, health) combined with enemy encounters in confined spaces
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a prototype T-800 infiltrator completing tactical missions before Judgment Day
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed action platformer that prioritizes combat over exploration; uncompromising difficulty with minimal concessions to player convenience. Designed as straightforward level-based progression without narrative framing devices.

difficulty.continues_system: Standard NES continue system allowing replay from game over
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No documented difficulty settings or accessibility options in available sources

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon pickups found during levels (ammunition and firearm types)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level advancement; hospital level confirmed as story location
replayability_hooks: High score table (intended feature, re-enableable via Game Genie in European version)
Score-based progression encourages replaying levels for better performance

world_building_techniques: Level design environments (hospital setting) convey mission context
Unused graffiti graphics in prototype suggest environmental storytelling was considered but removed

danger_signals: Combat-centric design rewards aggressive play and weapon acquisition
Hospital setting in Terminator universe context implies infiltration/elimination mission
environmental_storytelling: Hospital level layout conveys confined tactical environment
Removed graffiti elements suggest developers intended environmental atmosphere building

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Terminator 2: Judgment Day (NES) article

assessment_notes.data_quality: Source material is primarily about unused content, prototype differences, and hidden developer credits. Limited explicit documentation of core design philosophy, difficulty tuning rationale, or progression design intentions. Game is characterized in source as 'terrible game based on the Terminator series,' suggesting poor critical reception.
assessment_notes.missing_data: Boss design patterns, specific difficulty philosophy, player agency options, and detailed progression mechanics not documented in available source. Would benefit from gameplay documentation or developer interviews.
